rulings.Case Title: ELLEN HOKE VS. JAMES W. JONES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 20, 2013, Line 9: Defendant JAMES JONES' MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT Defendant's motion to set aside default and default judgment pursuant to CCP 473 is denied. Defendant did not timely file the motion within six months of the clerk's entry of default on October 19, 2012. (See Rutan v. Summit Sports, Inc. (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 965, 970.) Even if the motion to set aside default judgment is timely, Defendant has not met his burden of showing that the default judgment was the result of his inadvertence or excusable neglect. (See CCP § 473(b).) Finally, to the extent Plaintiff contends that he was not served with the summons and complaint, the evidence does not support this position. The registered process server describes in detail the service on plaintiff on September 16, 2012 at 1:45 p.m. (Declaration of Andy Esquer, paragraphs 1-3). This is corroborated by the Declaration of Plaintiff Ellen Hoke, who states that about 30 minutes later, she received a voicemail from Defendant suggesting they try to "resolve issues between us," and that Defendant did not "have the time or the patience to be trying to go through legalities or some bullshit." Hoke Decl. 5. An inference can be drawn from the Hoke declaration, when read in its entirety, that Defendant had just been served with the summons and complaint, as set forth in the Esquer Declaration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEVENS VS. JOHN MILLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Complainants Touraj Boussina'S And Perry Boussina'S Motion To Compel Mediation/Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 20, 2013, Line 22: Cross Complainants Touraj Boussina's and Perry Boussina's Motion To Compel Mediation/Arbitration Denied. Defendants Touraj Boussina and Perry Boussina ("Boussinas") have not established that they were not parties to the subscription agreement containing the arbitration clause. Generally, "someone not a party to the arbitration agreement cannot be compelled to arbitrate." (Sky Sports, Inc. v. Superior Court (2011) 201 Cal.App.4th 1363, 1367.) Further, the causes of action at issue arise out of the work performed on the bar by Plaintiff which he contends unjustly enriched the Bousinnas. rulings.Case Title: JOSELITO LAURORA et al VS. RAM K PARMAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Quash Deft'S Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 20, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFFs JOSELITO LAURORA and MERLYN MACAHILAS' Motion To Quash Defendant's Subpoenas. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. The records to be produced pursuant to the subpoena to Kaiser shall be limited to records since 2011 concerning Plaintiff Macahila's Inpingement Syndrome; records during September of 2012 concerning her heavy bleeding and cancer diagnosis; and records since September 2011 concerning psychiatric or psychological information. The records to be produced pursuant to the subpoena to Catholic Charities CYO shall be limited to payroll and time records from September 2011 to the present and documents regarding Plaintiff's medical condition and any request for a leave of absence or time off from Catholic Charities. The parties shall meet and confer concerning a stipulated protective order. Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint alleges that Defendant Parmer failed to provide Plaintiff Macahilas sick/medical leave in or around September 2011 when Mrs. Macahilas was diagnosed with Impingement Syndrome (FAC Par. 34). It also alleges that Defendant Parmer fired Mrs. Macahilas when she requested that she be allowed to leaver her shift early on the morning of September 23, 2012 to seek medical attention at Kaiser as a result of heavy bleeding that was not subsiding (FAC Par. 38). Portions of the Kaiser medical records are directly relevant to Plaintiff's claims and essential to the fair resolution of the lawsuit. Britt v. Sup.Ct. (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 859. Defendants are entitled to discovery concerning psychiatric or psychological information in Plaintiff's medical records since September 2011. The sixth cause of action of Plaintiffs' complaint asserts a cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress against all defendants. The case relied upon by Plaintiff, Tilo v. Sup.Ct. (1997) 55 Cal. App. 4th 1379-1382-83, does not control because the plaintiff in that case did not assert a cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: OMNIMAGNET, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. JEFFREY L. GOODMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Omnimagnet, Llc, A Delaware Limited Liability Company'S Motion For Entry Of A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 20, 2013, Line 5, Plaintiff Omnimagnet, LLC's Motion For Entry Of A Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. The proposed protective order provides a mechanism for handling of material designated for protection by any party, and for a party to challenge any other party's designation of material for protection. The court will enter Plaintiff Omnimagnet's proposed protective order with the following modification: "Modification - Provided that nothing in this order determines that any document marked "CONFIDENTIAL" or "ATTORNEYS' EYES ONLY" qualifies for sealing under CRC 2.550 or relieves any party from following the procedure in CRC 2.551." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: OMNIMAGNET, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. JEFFREY L. GOODMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Omnimagnet, Llc, A Delaware Limited Liability Company'S Motion For Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 20, 2013, Line 5, Plaintiff Omnimagnet, LLC's Motion For Attorney Fees. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. It would be helpful to the court for the parties to be prepared to address their positions on the following factors enumerated in Moran v. Endres (2006) 135 Cal. App. 4th 952, 955. Did the possible recovery against defendant Omnimagnet change as a result of the court's order striking Plaintiff's third cause of action for retaliation? Did the factual allegations which Omnimagnet has to defend change? Did the work involved in trying the case change? Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: OMNIMAGNET, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. JEFFREY L. GOODMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Deft To Compel Pltf'S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories; Request For Admissions, Special Interrogatories, And Demand To Indentify And Produce Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 20, 2013, Line 5, CROSS COMPLAINANT JEFFREY GOODMAN's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Further Responses To Form Interrogatories; Request For Admissions, Special Interrogatories, And Demand To Identify And Produce Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Defendant Jeffrey Goodman's motion is timely. Plaintiff OmniMagnet filed a special motion to strike under CCP Section 425.16 on June 11, 2013 - the 15th day after serving its responses. Movant Goodman filed his motion 10 days after entry of the court's September 30, 20133 order on the special motion to strike. Excluding the 111 days when all discovery proceedings were stayed pursuant to CCP Section 425.16(g), Defendant filed his motion within the 45-day time periods set forth in CCP Sections 2030.300(c), 2031.310(c), and 2033.290(c). Omnimagnet's assertion of blanket general objections and refusal to provide any substantive responses to even a single interrogatory, request for production, or request for admission constitutes a misuse of the discovery process under CCP Section 2023.030(a) and is without substantial justification. However, the court finds that imposition of sanctions against Omnimagnet would be unjust in light of Defendant Goodman's refusal to consent to entry of a stipulated protective order governing the treatment of information that a party designates for protection. Nevertheless, the court finds that Defendant Goodman complied with his obligation to meet and confer pursuant to CCP Section 2023.020. His attorney's June 6, 2013 meet and confer letter constituted a good-faith attempt to resolve informally the substantive discovery dispute. Within 30 days Plaintiff Omnimagnet shall serve verified code-compliant responses to Defendant Goodman's Form Interrogatories; Requests for Admissions; Special Interrogatories; and Demand to Identify and Produce Documents, and shall produce responsive documents to Defendant Goodman. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHUN YUN VS. JOE'S UNION L.P., DBA ORIGINAL JOE'S et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents From Defendant; Request To Deem Plaintiff'S Request For Admission Admiatted; And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 20, 2013, Line 6, PLAINTIFF CHUN YUN's Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents From Defendant; Request To Deem Plaintiff's Request For Admission Admitted; And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. It would be helpful to the court if the parties were prepared to address the following question at the hearing. Has Defendant served, prior to the hearing, responses to the discovery that is the subject of Plaintiff's motion? Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA A HESS VS. EXPERIAN CREDIT REPORTING INFO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 20, 2013, Line 18: DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained, with 20 days leave to amend, as to the third cause of action for accounting, and overruled as to the demurrers to the entire Complaint, the first cause of action for violation of Civ. Code §§ 1785, 1786.20, and the second cause of action for declaratory relief. Defendant Bank of America concedes that Plaintiff has not asserted the first and second causes of action against it. (Points & Auth, p. 3:24- 26, 4:7-9; Reply, p. 2:7-8.) For the third cause of action, Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for accounting. "To state a cause of action, only the simplest pleading is required: (1) The fiduciary relationship or other circumstances appropriate to the remedy [citation], [and] (2) A balance due from the defendant to the plaintiff that can only be ascertained by an accounting." (5 Witkin, Cal. Proc. (5th ed. 2008) Pleading, § 820, p. 236.) Plaintiff does not allege that a balance is due from Defendant Bank of America to Plaintiff, but rather that Plaintiff owes an amount to Defendant Bank of America. (Complaint, 17.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM
